items.title: CMS guidance: federal Medicaid/CHIP matching limits for certain noncitizens take effect Oct. 1, 2026
items.summary: CMS issued implementation guidance on new statutory limits to federal matching funds for Medicaid and CHIP for individuals who are not U.S. citizens or nationals, effective October 1, 2026. Exceptions include emergency medical conditions and lawfully residing children/pregnant people under existing state options.

items.title: HUD PIH 2026-05 ends EHV/Stability Voucher documentation flexibilities; PHAs have 60 days to comply
items.summary: HUD PIH Notice 2026-05 rescinds prior flexibilities allowing self-certification and other streamlined documentation for Emergency Housing Vouchers (EHV) and Stability Vouchers (SV). PHAs must update their administrative plans and comply within 60 days of issuance.

items.title: USDA tracks state SNAP purchase-restriction waivers: Texas, Florida, Colorado start in April 2026
items.summary: USDA's official tracker lists approved state SNAP food-restriction waivers with 2026 effective dates: Texas (April 1), Florida (April 20), Colorado (April 30), Arkansas (July 1). Restrictions vary but generally cover sodas, candy, and certain prepared foods. Kansas and Ohio waivers begin in 2027.

items.title: SSA 2026 COLA at 2.8%; SSI federal max is $994/month (individual), $1,491 (couple)
items.summary: SSA confirmed a 2.8% cost-of-living adjustment for 2026 affecting about 75 million Americans. The federal SSI payment standard is $994/month for individuals and $1,491/month for couples. Disability work thresholds are $1,690/month (non-blind SGA) and $2,830/month (blind SGA).

items.title: WIC FY2026 fruit/veg cash benefit: $26 (kids), $48 (preg/postpartum), $52 (breastfeeding)
items.summary: USDA FNS set FY2026 monthly WIC cash-value voucher amounts at $26 for children, $48 for pregnant/postpartum participants, and $52 for fully/mostly breastfeeding participants. States must authorize at least one form of fruits/vegetables beyond fresh (frozen, canned, and/or dried) by April 2026.

items.title: IRS: EITC/ACTC refunds were available by March 2 for most early filers; extension deadline October 15
items.summary: Per the PATH Act, the IRS could not issue refunds for returns claiming EITC or ACTC before mid-February. Most early filers using direct deposit who filed online with no errors received refunds by March 2, 2026. Filing extensions to October 15, 2026 must have been requested by April 15, 2026.

items.title: HHS/ACF proposes reallotment of FY2025 LIHEAP funds; Federal Register comments due April 30, 2026
items.summary: The Administration for Children and Families published a Federal Register notice proposing reallotment of unspent FY2025 LIHEAP funds to jurisdictions that received FY2026 direct LIHEAP awards. The comment period ended April 30, 2026.

items.title: FAFSA federal deadlines: June 30, 2026 for 2025–26; June 30, 2027 for 2026–27
items.summary: USA.gov lists the federal FAFSA submission deadline for the 2025–26 school year as June 30, 2026, and the 2026–27 deadline as June 30, 2027. States and individual schools often set earlier deadlines for state and institutional aid programs.

items.title: CMS: Medicaid/CHIP enrollment at 75.7 million in December 2025, down 4.7% from year ago
items.summary: December 2025 data shows 68.5 million enrolled in Medicaid and 7.2 million in CHIP, totaling 75.7 million. Enrollment is down about 1.0% from November 2025 (727,000 people) and 4.7% from December 2024.
